notes: Open-source OS kernel; dominant enterprise server OS by 2015 (>70% market share); basis for Android cloud computing and AI infrastructure.
Text editor and extensible computing environment; David Sifry (Linuxcare CTO) contributor.
SMB/CIFS implementation for Linux/Unix enabling Windows file/print sharing; Andrew Tridgell (Linuxcare) original author; remains widely deployed.
Office productivity suite for Linux; Linuxcare provided support; Sun open-sourced as OpenOffice.org; later forked to LibreOffice.
Predecessor to Linux; IT management had Unix support contracts in place; Linux positioned as Unix alternative in enterprise.
Primary competitor to Linux in enterprise server market at time of writing; Linux described as alternative to Unix and Windows NT.
Free online knowledge base provided by Linuxcare; all services available via private-branding (e.g. www.dell.linuxcare.com).
Training arm of Linuxcare offering Linux System Administration Network Administration Security Linux for NT Professionals Extreme Samba Linux for Executives courses.
